We are often so busy that we forget the gratitude we owe and the commitments we have made, not only to our friends but to God himself. But truly godly people will find no peace until they fulfill those obligations. And the most deserving recipients of kindness and charity will usually not be found without investigation. Jonathan was David's sworn friend, so he shows kindness to his son Mephibosheth. God is faithful to us; let us not be unfaithful to one another. If Providence has lifted us up, and our friends and their families are brought low, we must see that as a better chance for us to be kind to them.

Because David was a type of Christ, his Lord and Son, his Root and Offspring, may his kindness to Mephibosheth remind us of the kindness and love of God our Savior to fallen people, to whom he owed no obligation, as David did to Jonathan. The Son of God seeks this lost and ruined race, who have not sought after him. He comes to seek and to save them!
